invariants.label: IPC Channel Uniqueness
invariants.description: Each ipcMain.handle channel must be registered exactly once across the entire Electron process. Duplicate handlers will crash the app and break persistence.

invariants.label: Local-First Data Storage
invariants.description: All persistent user data must be stored locally in the user data directory. No user data may be transmitted to remote servers without explicit user consent and configuration.

invariants.label: Telemetry Non-Blocking
invariants.description: Telemetry and audit log emissions must never block the main inference or IPC response path. All telemetry calls are fire-and-forget with caught errors.

invariants.label: Error Non-Fatal in Non-Critical Paths
invariants.description: Errors in non-critical subsystems (reflection, maintenance, telemetry, astro) must not crash the app or block the user. All non-critical paths use try/catch with logged recovery.

invariants.label: No Plaintext Secret Logging
invariants.description: API keys, tokens, passwords, and other secrets must never be written to log files, telemetry payloads, or IPC responses. All log paths use the redact() sanitizer.

invariants.label: Context Bridge Security
invariants.description: The Electron contextBridge must not expose raw ipcRenderer, Node.js APIs, or event objects to the renderer. Only whitelisted, purpose-bound functions may be exposed via window.tala.

invariants.label: Identity Consistency
invariants.description: Tala's core identity, values, and behavioral boundaries must remain consistent across sessions and mode transitions. Identity state is never silently reset by inference or tool calls.

invariants.label: Inference Fallback Ordering
invariants.description: When the primary inference provider is unavailable, the system must attempt fallback providers in the configured priority order before returning an error. No silent drops are permitted.

invariants.label: Memory Write Isolation
invariants.description: Memory writes must be scoped to the active session or workspace. Cross-session writes require explicit context justification and are logged with their source.

invariants.label: Audit Log Completeness
invariants.description: All user-initiated actions, inference invocations, and tool executions must produce at least one audit log entry. Silent execution is not permitted for any action that modifies state.
